title: Schenor

{{Quote|Aside from having seen troopers murder his parents and a couple dozen other colonists, being stuck inside a spaceship with a bunch of us ugly aliens might be making him nervous.|A Rebel agent assigned by Luke Skywalker to take A'kazz home|<ref name="RPG" />}}
The '''Schenor''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of tall [[Feline/Legends|felines]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rhamsis Callo]]. They upheld a code of honor and a respect for the [[Unarmed combat/Legends|martial arts]], and lived in a stratified society with noble, middle, and peasant classes. All Schenor were [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]].

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|Just that he gives me the creeps, sitting there staring at me all the time with those bulging yellow eyes…|A Rebel agent assigned to take A'kazz home|<ref name="RPG" />}}
The Schenor (scientific name '''''Schenor sapiens''''') were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of 8 [[Foot/Legends|foot]]-tall [[Feline/Legends|felinoids]] with large yellow [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. All members of the species were [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]. The young were referred to as "hatchlings," suggesting an oviparous reproductive system.<ref name="RPG" />

==Society and culture==
Schenor had a strong respect for the [[Unarmed combat/Legends|martial arts]]. They held an unyielding honor code, a deep commitment to their families, and a traditional distrust of outsiders. Schenor grouped themselves in social units known as [[nest-guild]]s, led by a [[nest-guildmaster]]. Their society was highly stratified, with Schenor nobility maintaining great power over the servile peasant and middle classes.<ref name="RPG" />

The Schenor's technology was advanced enough to create [[reaction car]]s. Those cars were large, noisy, and inefficient [[Hovercraft|ground-effect vehicles]] that were fast and powerful, but not particularly safe or nimble. The architecture on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Rhamsis Callo]], was dominated by red [[Sandstone/Legends|sandstone]] decorated with [[Clay/Legends|clay]] ornamentation in earth-tone geometric patterns. The species also maintained a presence away from Rhamsis Callo, including a [[Schenor research station|research station]] a great distance from their homeworld staffed by several dozen individuals.<ref name="RPG" />

==History==
{{Quote|Well, the kid must've seen something important, or Skywalker wouldn't be sending us half-way across the galaxy to deliver him to his people.|A Rebel agent assigned to take A'kazz home|<ref name="RPG" />}}
The Schenor evolved on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Rhamsis Callo,<ref name="RPG" /> in the [[Danjar sector/Legends|Danjar sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> The Schenor had maintained neutrality during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], avoiding allying with either the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] or the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]], although they eventually allowed the Empire to begin construction of a base on their homeworld.<ref name="RPG" /> However, at some point between the [[Evacuation of Yavin]] and the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]],<ref name="dating note">"[[The Silent Witness]]" refers to "[[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Skywalker]]," who was given promotion to that [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] after the [[evacuation of Yavin]]; and it refers to Rebels and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]], indicating it occurred before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].</ref> a research station operated by the Schenor came under attack by a heterogeneous group of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]].<ref name="RPG" />

{{RPG}}
A team of Rebel agents responded to the station's distress call, only to find an ongoing massacre of station personnel. After a spirited firefight, an Imperial [[Gunship/Legends|gunboat]] pinned down the Rebels and collected the mercenaries, leaving before the Rebels could track it. Inside the facility, they found only a single survivor of the dozens of Schenor stationed there, a juvenile named [[A'kazz]].<ref name="RPG" />

The Rebel team delivered A'kazz to a nearby Alliance base, where [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] questioned the Schenor hatchling privately. Believing A'kazz's condition would lead the Schenor to side with the Rebellion, Skywalker directed the Rebel agents to return the young Schenor to his nest-guild. The agents landed at the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] of [[Chafflock]] on Rhamsis Callo and departed there by reaction car, avoiding being waylaid by local Imperial forces assigned to the partially-completed base.<ref name="RPG" />

The Rebel agents delivered A'kazz to [[B'karitz]], the nest-guildmaster of A'kazz's nest-guild and a figure of influence in a powerful Callo province. Initially suspicious of the agents, B'karitz soon discerned that they were not seeking ransom for the hatchling and questioned the youth in a [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] trance. B'karitz was appalled to learn of the trauma inflicted on A'kazz and vowed to break the [[Schenor/Imperial treaty|Empire's treaty]] with his people and drive them from the world. B'karitz, his nest-warriors, and the agents went to the Imperial base, where B'karitz declared to the [[Unidentified Imperial ambassador to Rhamsis Callo|Imperial ambassador]] that the treaty was null and void and that the Imperials must leave. The Imperials pretended to cooperate, but only long enough for a detachment of [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] to surround the group and attack. The besieged group was able to break through the encircling forces and make their way to the Rebels' [[Starship/Legends|ship]], from which B'karitz was able to summon aid from other Schenor nobles. The Imperials attempted to flee, but the Rebels were able to disable and board the [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador's]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], returning the ambassador to the planet for local judicial action.<ref name="RPG" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Schenor appeared in "[[The Silent Witness]]," an adventure idea from the [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|first edition]] of ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that was published in [[1987]]. The adventure describes them as "eight-foot samurai catmen."<ref name="RPG" />
